feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Cloud Identity Services
feed.subtitle: SAP Cloud Identity Services Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: showing access denied issue while trying to alunch SAP Cloud Identity Service
entry.content: After i setup my password, i am trying to launch Sap Cloud identity Service getting Access Denied error

entry.title: Feasibility & Architecture: Centralized Governance for Hybrid .NET (Azure) with IAG, SCIM
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am designing a centralized Identity and Access Management (IAM) and governance strategy for a hybrid multi-cloud landscape, and I want to validate the absolute technical feasibility and best practices for this setup.

Our Target Requirement

We aim to establish a centralized administration system to manage, provision, and audit user identities, roles, and access across both our core SAP landscape (S/4HANA Cloud, SAP BTP) and non-SAP custom applications ( .NET apps and Java apps).

The primary business and compliance goals are:

  1. Single Administration Platform: All user lifecycle adjustments (onboarding, offboarding, modifications) must be handled from a single platform.

  2. Unified Access Governance: Centralized execution of workflows, risk analytics/Segregation of Duties (SoD) checks, and compliant access provisioning across SAP and non-SAP boundaries.

  3. Dynamic API Authorization: For our custom .NET app on Azure, authentication will run via Azure AD (Microsoft Entra ID), but granular authorization (CRUD validations) must map dynamically to assigned enterprise role metrics.

Proposed High-Level Architecture Component Layout

  • Access Governance: SAP Cloud Identity Access Governance (IAG).

  • Identity Lifecycle & Provisioning Engine: SAP Cloud Identity Services – Identity Provisioning (IPS).

  • Identity Federation / Token Proxy: SAP Cloud Identity Services – Identity Authentication (IAS) mapped to SAP BTP XSUAA.

  • Non-SAP Endpoint target: Custom .NET apps, Java apps.

 

Our Specific Queries for SAP Experts

1. Architectural Feasibility & Non-SAP App Connectors

  • Is it fully feasible to utilize SAP IAG as the master governance authority to provision and control user entitlements for a standalone custom web API deployed outside the SAP perimeter (Azure)?

  • Given that IAG natively supports standard SAP Cloud applications out-of-the-box (e.g., S/4HANA Cloud, Ariba, SuccessFactors), what is the recommended design pattern to register an external custom .NET endpoint as a recognized Target System within the governance framework?

2. Where Exactly Does SCIM 2.0 Apply?

  • We intend to use the System for Cross-Domain Identity Management (SCIM 2.0) protocol standard. Does SAP IAG communicate identity schemas directly via SCIM, or does it orchestrate the target provisioning lifecycle implicitly by delegating to SAP Identity Provisioning Service (IPS) as its execution engine?

  • To map custom security attributes to our .NET app (e.g., distinguishing between a view-only Viewer and a read-write Admin), should the SCIM 2.0 target schema extension configuration reside inside SAP IPS transformations or directly in the IAS identity directory configuration?

3. Necessary Customization within SAP IAG

  • What specific configurations or customizations are required within SAP IAG (such as the Access Request or Role Design modules) to ingest custom repository boundaries, define non-SAP technical permission roles, and run Segregation of Duties (SoD) risk analysis over custom application actions?

  • How do we construct custom repository structures within IAG when the target system does not offer a native metadata connector endpointt

I would appreciate any insights, architectural blueprints, or implementation guidance from anyone who has deployed a similar hybrid infrastructure using SAP IAG/IPS for third-party endpoints.

Thanks in advance!

entry.title: My Profile button in Success Factors
entry.content:
Good morning,

We kindly request your assistance in clarifying why there are different names on the profile button for the employee themselves, depending on whether or not they are a manager. If the user is a manager, the button shows “Employee File”; if not, it shows “My Profile.” How can we change this?

Thank you.

entry.title: SID Deactivate in Public Cloud/IAS
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

We are using SAP S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition with IAS/CBC and S4HC. For employee offboarding, we are considering the following process to deactive SID instead of delete SID, for we are afraid if we delete, it will influence IAS/S4 system inconsistent.

Process as following:

1. Set user status to Inactive in IAS

Status: Inactive
Valid To: End of Valid Date
(eg 2026/06/16)

2. Remove user groups in IAS

All unassign(clear all group)

3. Synchronize changes to CBC in IAS(IPS)

4. Maintain Worker work type valid date in APP "Manage Workforce" in S/4HANA Cloud
Worker Types End date:
End of Valid Date
(eg. 2026/06/16)

5. Lock the business user in APP "Maintain Business User" in S/4HANA Cloud

Click "Lock" indicator

6. Remove all assigned business roles in APP "Maintain Business User" in S/4HANA Cloud

Remove all Business Role

Q1: For sure following step is correct or SAP recommended approach is delete SID? 

Q2: If you complete the steps above, the SID account will no longer occupy FUE?

Thanks.

Tina

entry.title: Subject: Unable to Access SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS) Administration Console – /admin/forbidde
entry.content:
Hello Team,

I am facing an issue while accessing the SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS) Administration Console in an SAP BTP Trial account.

Steps Performed:

  1. Created a new SAP BTP Trial account.

  2. Created a new subaccount.

  3. Assigned administrator role collections to my user, including:

    • Subaccount Administrator

    • Subaccount Service Administrator

  4. Added the Cloud Identity Services entitlement.

  5. Subscribed to Cloud Identity Services using the default plan.

  6. Received the "Welcome to SAP Cloud Identity Services" activation email.

  7. Clicked the activation link and successfully activated the account.

  8. Set the password successfully.

  9. Configured Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) successfully.

  10. Opened Cloud Identity Services using "Go To Application".

Trust Configuration Tests:

  • Tested with the Default Identity Provider (Active).

  • Also configured and tested with a Custom Identity Provider.

  • The same issue occurs in both scenarios.

Expected Result:

I should be able to access the IAS Administration Console.

Actual Result:

After successful authentication and MFA configuration, I am redirected to:

/admin/forbidden.html

The page displays the following message:

"Forbidden"

"Sorry, but you are not authorized to access the requested content."

"You entered a valid URL, but you are not authorized to view the content. Please contact your administrator for more information."

The URL is successfully reached, but access to the IAS Administration Console is denied.

Additional Information:

  • Authentication is successful.

  • MFA setup is successful.

  • Cloud Identity Services subscription status is "Subscribed".

  • BTP administrator role collections are assigned to my user.

  • The issue occurs after successful account activation, password setup, MFA configuration, and login.

  • The same issue occurs in two different SAP BTP Trial accounts created with two different email addresses.

  • The issue persists regardless of whether the Default Identity Provider or a Custom Identity Provider is configured.

Additional Observation:

Since the activation email is received, the account is activated successfully, MFA is configured successfully, and login succeeds, authentication appears to be working correctly. However, access to the IAS Administration Console is denied with /admin/forbidden.html.

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: Display access to Provisioning logs in Cloud Identity Services
entry.content: I am exploring the feasibility of providing display (read-only) access to provisioning logs in SAP Cloud Identity Services. Currently, SAP does not offer a dedicated read-only sub-role specifically for log access; however, is it possible to design a custom role to meet this requirement?Please advise

entry.title: Unable to Access Cloud Identity Services Admin Console – "You are not authorized to view the content
entry.content:
Hello Team,

I am using an SAP BTP Trial account and have recently set up Cloud Identity Services.

The steps I completed are:

Created an SAP BTP Trial account and subaccount.
Enabled Cloud Identity Services from the BTP cockpit.
Established trust between the BTP subaccount and Cloud Identity Services.
Received the activation email and successfully activated the Cloud Identity Services tenant using the activation link.

Issue: After activation, when I am redirected to the Cloud Identity Services administration portal, I receive the following error message:

"You are not authorized to view the content. Please contact your administrator."

In some cases, the page also displays a "Forbidden" error.

The authentication process itself appears to complete successfully, and I can log in with my credentials. However, after authentication, I am unable to access the administration console.

What is confusing is that I am the administrator who activated the tenant from the activation email, so I expected to have administrative access to the Cloud Identity Services tenant.

Additional Information:

Environment: SAP BTP Trial
Cloud Identity Services tenant activated successfully
Trust configuration completed in BTP
Issue occurs immediately after authentication and redirection to the admin console
Has anyone experienced a similar issue with a trial account? Is there an additional role assignment or configuration step required after tenant activation?

Any guidance would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you

entry.title: Trial Cloud Identity Service instance: access denied
entry.content:
I'm getting access denied error when I try to open the CIS tenant launched in the BTP trial account. It's been failing for more than a week. I've already tried to recreate the instance, but it doesn't work.

Can anyone help fix this issue or at least point me to the right channel to report BTP trial account issues?

 

 

Thanks.

entry.title: Support required for Onboarding New hires using the SAP Identity Authentication Service
entry.content:
Dear Team,

We require your support in implementing the SAP Identity Authentication Service (IAS) for onboarding new hires. Currently, we are facing some confusion regarding the existing API being used and the appropriate approach for its implementation.

We would appreciate your guidance in reviewing the current setup and advising on the correct integration approach, along with any recommended best practices.

Looking forward to your support.

Regards,
Srinanda

entry.title: PRODUCTION IAS HOW TO CREATE MASS USERS WITHOUT BLOCK USERS
entry.content:
While creating mass users in IAS 

1.mass users able to create but users are blocking.
2.how unblock mass users in IAS.

entry.title: Importing users failed. Error is: Missing required displayName attribute
entry.content:
Dear Sap 

we discovered we are unable to impot users from csv files in Sap ID service

Since one week at least  we noted the import of these csv files exported from Sap IBP  fails  with the error:

First 0 users are imported. User import has been interrupted for users with email address "name.surname@xxxx.com". The reason for this error is as follows: "Failed to create user in IdDS: {"schemas":["urn:ietf:params:scim:api:messages:2.0:Error"],"status":"400","scimType":"invalidValue","detail":"Missing required displayName attribute."}".

Not clear at all the reason of the error  , we always used this method since years.

We found note 3240210 , seems exacttly the issue

BUt again, we need to clarify what is changed and why , for sure nodoby of us  had set this field as Requested .

 

Regards

Roberto

entry.title: Unexpected Connections Consumption in SAP Build Work Zone Standard Edition
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community,

We are currently implementing a Supplier Portal using SAP Build Work Zone, Standard Edition.

Our current subscription includes:

1,000 Active Users
0 Connections

Authentication is performed through SAP Cloud Identity Services (Identity Authentication - IAS) as our Identity Provider (IdP).

Due to an omission during the commercial contracting process, our subscription was provisioned with Active Users only, while the Connections metric was not included. We are already discussing this situation with our SAP Account Team, but we would like to better understand both the technical behavior and whether anyone has experienced a similar situation.

During our testing, we observed that every external login generates Connections, even though our subscription does not include this metric.

From a technical perspective, we would like to know:

1. Is the generation of the Connections metric mandatory in SAP Build Work Zone Standard Edition?
2. Is there any way to completely prevent the generation of the Connections metric through Role Collections, authorizations or SAP Build Work Zone configuration?

From a commercial perspective, we would also appreciate guidance from anyone who has experienced a similar situation.

Any guidance or shared experience would be greatly appreciated.

Thank you.

entry.title: Unique employee number based user creation in Identity directory service
entry.content: How to create users based on unique employee number in Identity directory service?

entry.title: AMS: can authorization policies be combined/shared across multiple CAP apps?
entry.content:
Hi experts,

I'm exploring how AMS handles authorization across several independent CAP apps. Each app has its own IAS application and deploys its own DCL base policies. My goal is to understand whether one assignment can cover policies from multiple apps.

What I tried:

  1. Reuse service – From the documentation I couldn't work out how a CAP app is actually linked to a reuse service. Setting consumed-services on the consuming app's IAS instance had no effect: the reuse app didn't appear under "Reuse services consumed by this application", and no reuse dropdown showed in the consuming app's Authorization Policies tab.
  2. Combine Authorization Policies (Add Combination) – Only policies from the same application are selectable.

The docs say a combined policy can "bundle authorization policies within or across solutions":

Questions:

  • Is combining/sharing policies across applications supported for custom CAP apps, or only for SAP-delivered bundled solutions?
  • If supported, what establishes the relationship so another app's base policies become selectable?
  • If not, is the intended pattern to centralize assignment via Identity Directory groups (SCIM2/IPS)?

entry.title: BTP Trail account: Cloud identity services Access denier error
entry.content:
Hi Team,

While accessing the Cloud identity services from BTP Trail account getting 

"You entered a valid URL, but you are not authorized to view the content; contact your system administrator"

Getting this error for quite some time, was there any issue in CIS?

Kindly assist..

Thanks in advance.

Regards,

Venkat

entry.title: Story Report Query
entry.content:
Hi Team,

I am trying to create a Story report of talent information or talent flags fields (potential, risk of loss, retention risk, mobility) etc . I am unable to find the schema for the same. Could you please suggest the schema.Also let me know if we cannot create Story Report for these fields now. Please let me know when this enhancement is planned.

entry.title: Higher Duty Assignment should be made primary for AD integration
entry.content:
For SF to AD integration, if an employee have multiple profiles with the standard profile, and multiple Higher duty profiles, whenever a Higher duty profile is active, then the higher duty profile should be taken as a primary profile instead of the standard profile. 

Any suggestions to achieve this

entry.title: Inquiry about reading nationalId from SuccessFactors and updating it in Cloud Identity Services
entry.content: I want to read the nationalId from SuccessFactors using Identity Provisioning Service and update it in the Login name (UserName) field in Cloud Identity Services. In SuccessFactors, the nationalId is available under PerNationalId. In IPS, I added the attributes 'personKeyNav,PerNationalId/nationalId' to sf.user.attributes.expand and included 'PerNationalId' in sf.user.attributes. I am using the following source transformation in IPS: { "sourcePath": "perNationalIdNav/results[0]/nationalId", "targetPath": "$.urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:2.0:User:" } And in the Cloud Identity Services transformation, I have: { "sourcePath": "$.urn:ietf:params:scim:schemas:extension:sap:2.0:User:", "targetPath": "$.userName", "correlationAttribute": true } I want to confirm if these configurations are correct for reading nationalId from SuccessFactors and updating it into the UserName field in Cloud Identity Services. I am requesting to open a support case for further assistance.

entry.title: Configuration SSO between SAC to S/4HANA
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

I am planning to establish Single Sign-On (SSO) between SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) and SAP S/4HANA, as well as between SAP SuccessFactors and SAP S/4HANA (where SAP S/4HANA is deployed on a Private Cloud). Azure AD work as identity provider and SAP IAS work as proxy

I would appreciate your guidance on the overall setup, as I have a few questions and would like to clarify some points:

  1. We currently have multiple SAP Identity Authentication Service (IAS) and Identity Provisioning Service (IPS) tenants provisioned by SAP. For example:

    • Landscape A (Test): IAS (Ariba Applications) and IPS (Ariba Applications, BTP, BIZX)
    • Landscape B (Production): Similar bundles
    • Landscape C (Test): IAS (BTP, BIZX) and IPS (BTP, BIZX) and 2 more 

    My question is: If I create a new SAP BTP subaccount and subscribe to IAS, will a new IAS tenant be created automatically and any additional license count ? If I want to use one of the existing IAS tenants instead of creating a new one, what is the recommended approach during the subscription and configuration process?

  2. For establishing SSO between SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) and SAP S/4HANA, is it necessary to create a separate SAP BTP subaccount and subscribe to SAC within that subaccount, or can the existing BTP landscape be used?

I would appreciate any guidance or best practices regarding this architecture and configuration.

Thank you in advance for your support.

Regards Shahid

entry.title: Request to Enable Onboarding SSO with a Single Email Notification from Onboarding Email Services
entry.content:
Dear Team,

We have received a business requirement to enable Single Sign-On (SSO) for candidates during the Onboarding process.

The expectation is that only one email notification should be triggered from Onboarding Email Services, and this email should include the SSO login link for the candidate. No additional or separate system-generated emails should be sent as part of the onboarding initiation process.

Could you please confirm:

  1. Whether this configuration is supported in SAP SuccessFactors Onboarding.
  2. If supported, the required settings and configuration steps to ensure that only a single onboarding email is sent containing the SSO link.
  3. Any prerequisites, limitations, or best practices associated with this setup.
  4. Whether there are any known impacts on candidate access, authentication, or onboarding workflow communications.

We would appreciate your guidance on achieving this requirement and any relevant documentation or KBAs that can assist with the configuration.

Regards,

Swapna
